hero.title: 5 Days Best of Kota Kinabalu, Kundasang & Mari Mari.
hero.titleAccent: Highland Sunrise, Island Snorkelling & the Living Culture of Borneo's Five Tribes.
hero.intro: Sabah in five days — the full picture. Cool highland mornings at Kundasang with Mount Kinabalu on the horizon. Natural fish therapy in a jungle river. A full island day snorkelling through Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park's coral reefs and flying the Coral Flyer zipline over the sea. And the one experience that makes this package exceptional: Mari Mari Cultural Village, where Sabah's five major indigenous tribes — the Dusun, Rungus, Lundayeh, Bajau, and Murut — have built authentic longhouse replicas and offer blowpipe, fire-starting, and traditional tattoo demonstrations that visitors describe as the single best day of their Borneo trip.

overview.heading: 5 Days Sabah: Highland, Islands, Culture — the Most Complete Sabah Package Available
body: This five-day private Sabah package is the most comprehensive tour in the state — combining four distinct Sabah experiences that together give a complete picture of Borneo's most accessible corner: the cool highland world of Kundasang and Kinabalu National Park (UNESCO World Heritage), the island and reef life of Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park, the highland pastoral beauty of Desa Cow Farm and the natural river fish therapy of Bombon Marakau Village, and the living cultural experience of Mari Mari Cultural Village, where Sabah's five major indigenous ethnic groups are represented together for the first time in Southeast Asia.

Most Sabah tours focus on a single theme — highland, island, or cultural. This package refuses that compromise. Day 1 and Day 2 are devoted to Kundasang and the highland interior: Kinabalu National Park, the Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike, Desa Cow Farm, natural fish therapy, and the scenic coastal return via the Floating Mosque and Tanjung Aru. Day 3 is the full island day in Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — snorkelling at Sapi Island, the Coral Flyer zipline over the ocean, the Gaya Island sandbar proboscis monkey walk, and the beach and water sports at Manukan Island. Day 4 is Mari Mari Cultural Village, 25 minutes from KK city — a half-day immersion in the traditional life of the Dusun, Rungus, Lundayeh, Bajau, and Murut peoples, with live demonstrations of blowpipe dart-shooting, bamboo fire-starting, traditional tattoo art, and the spectacular Lansaran trampoline jumping performed by the Murut warriors. Day 5 is a cultural and shopping morning in Kota Kinabalu before departure.

What makes this package the definitive Sabah experience is Mari Mari Cultural Village. Built by descendants of the five ethnic groups themselves, it is not a museum exhibit or a staged performance — it is a living site where the same families who practise these traditions in daily life demonstrate them in their original longhouse context. It is the only place in all of Sabah where a visitor can encounter all five major indigenous groups in a single morning, with demonstrations conducted by people for whom these skills are genuine cultural inheritance, not theatrical reconstruction.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Sabah Package is the Most Complete in the State

items.title: Mari Mari Cultural Village — Sabah's Five Tribes in One Living Experience
items.body: Mari Mari Cultural Village, located approximately 25 minutes from Kota Kinabalu city, is the only place in Sabah — and the first of its kind in Southeast Asia — where all five major indigenous ethnic groups of the state are represented together: the Dusun/Kadazan (the largest group in Sabah, traditionally rice farmers and traders), the Rungus (known for their intricate beadwork and gong music), the Lundayeh (hunters and fishermen from the interior highlands near the Sarawak and Kalimantan borders), the Bajau (Sabah's 'sea cowboys', known equally for their horsemanship on land and their remarkable free-diving ability at sea), and the Murut (the last Sabahan tribe to renounce headhunting, known as fierce warriors and celebrated for the Lansaran, a traditional group trampoline made of bamboo slats). Each group's longhouse has been built by descendants of the original communities, using traditional materials and construction methods passed down through generations.
items.imageAlt: Mari Mari Cultural Village ethnic tribes longhouse Sabah Borneo traditional demonstration

items.title: Blowpipe, Fire-Starting & Lansaran — Living Demonstrations by Tribal Descendants
items.body: The demonstrations at Mari Mari Cultural Village are performed by people who have grown up with these skills within their own family traditions — not by actors hired from outside the community. The Dusun guide demonstrates traditional blowpipe dart-shooting with remarkable accuracy, using a handcrafted hardwood sumpitan and iron-tipped bamboo darts that can be driven through a plank at close range. The Rungus show bamboo fire-starting using the traditional flint method — producing flame from dry bamboo in under a minute. The Bajau demonstrate traditional tattoo art, a practice with specific spiritual and social significance in the tribe's culture. The Murut's Lansaran — a long platform of springy bamboo slats used for group jumping, traditionally performed after headhunting raids — is the most physically spectacular demonstration in the village, with participants bouncing several metres into the air. Visitors are invited to try most demonstrations under guidance.
items.imageAlt: Blowpipe dart shooting Lansaran trampoline Mari Mari Cultural Village Sabah traditional tribe

items.title: Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — Two Islands, One Unforgettable Day
items.body: The island day in this package covers Sapi Island and Manukan Island — two of the five protected islands in Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park, lying 15 to 40 minutes from Jesselton Jetty by speedboat. Sapi Island's fringing coral reef offers some of the most accessible snorkelling in Sabah, with colourful hard and soft corals, parrotfish, angelfish, and occasional reef sharks in the deeper channels. The Coral Flyer zipline — 250 metres of open ocean between Sapi and Gaya Islands at 60 km/h — is one of the few ocean ziplines in Southeast Asia and takes just 90 seconds to complete. On Gaya Island's sandbar, proboscis monkeys — Borneo's iconic endemic primates — frequently appear along the shoreline in the early afternoon. Manukan Island, with its 1,500-metre arc of white sand and calm clear water, adds a beach and water sports element to complement the snorkelling at Sapi.
items.imageAlt: Manukan Island white sand beach Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park Kota Kinabalu Sabah snorkelling

items.title: Kundasang Highland World — Sunrise, Cows and a Jungle River
items.body: Kundasang at 1,700 metres is the highland heart of this package: a landscape of cool misty mornings, rolling green hills, vegetable farms, and the granite peak of Mount Kinabalu (4,095m) dominating the horizon. The Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike — a gentle 10-minute walk to a 400-metre summit — delivers one of the most dramatic dawn views in Sabah, with the Crocker Range lit from behind as the sun rises over the peak. Desa Cow Farm below Kinabalu National Park, known as Sabah's 'Little New Zealand', is where Jersey cows graze on impossibly green pastures at altitude — the pastoral scenery is genuinely striking, and the opportunity to bottle-feed calves and taste fresh highland dairy products makes this a highlight for travellers of all ages. The natural river fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village rounds out the highland experience: wild Garra Rufa fish in the cold, fast-flowing jungle river provide an exfoliating experience that guests consistently describe as the most relaxing hour of their trip.
items.imageAlt: Kinabalu National Park Kundasang highland view Mount Kinabalu UNESCO World Heritage Sabah

itinerary.heading: Your 5-Day Best of Kota Kinabalu, Kundasang & Mari Mari Itinerary

days.title: Arrive Kota Kinabalu — Tamparuli — Nabalu — Kinabalu Park — Kundasang
days.description: Arrive at Kota Kinabalu International Airport and meet your private English-speaking driver-guide. The journey to Kundasang begins with a series of stops that introduce Sabah's cultural and natural character before you reach the highlands. Rumah Terbalik — the Upside Down House — is one of only five such inverted-architecture structures in the world, where furniture, fittings, and entire rooms are fixed to the ceiling, creating a playful optical experience that sets a memorable tone for the trip. Continue to Jambatan Tamparuli, the traditional long suspension bridge spanning the Tuaran River at Tamparuli town, swaying gently underfoot as you cross above the river valley. At Pekan Nabalu's craft market, browse handwoven textiles, handmade crafts, and traditional goods sold by Kadazan-Dusun artisans — this is one of the best places in Sabah to purchase authentic indigenous crafts at fair prices. The final stop before Kundasang is Kinabalu National Park, where your guide leads a gentle nature trail through the lower montane forest — a UNESCO World Heritage ecosystem containing more plant species per unit area than anywhere else on earth, including carnivorous pitcher plants, rhododendrons in full flower, and orchids that grow from the mossy trunks of ancient trees. Check in to Hotel Benjamin, Kundasang, and step out onto the terrace as Mount Kinabalu catches the last golden light of the day above the tree line.
suggestedActivities: Rumah Terbalik (Upside Down House — 1 of 5 in the world)
Kinabalu National Park guided nature trail (UNESCO World Heritage)

days.title: Sosodikon Hill Sunrise — Desa Cow Farm — Fish Therapy — Return KK — Tanjung Aru Sunset
days.description: An early start before dawn for the Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike, a gentle 10-minute walk to a 400-metre summit with one of Sabah's most breathtaking panoramas: Mount Kinabalu's granite peak and the full sweep of the Crocker Range lit by the rising sun, while the highland valleys below are still filled with morning mist at temperatures around 15°C to 17°C. Return to Hotel Benjamin for a full breakfast before departing for Desa Cow Farm, known throughout Malaysia as 'Sabah's Little New Zealand' — a working dairy farm at 1,700 metres altitude where Jersey cows graze on rolling green hills against a backdrop of mountain forest, and where guests can bottle-feed young calves, taste chilled fresh dairy products, and photograph the cows and their mountain surroundings. The scenic resemblance to New Zealand's hill country is genuine enough that the comparison has become a permanent part of the farm's identity. After the farm, continue to Bombon Marakau Village in Ranau district for the natural river fish therapy: a clear, cold mountain stream populated by wild Garra Rufa fish that congregate around visitors wading into the river and begin their cleaning work naturally. The combination of the cold highland water and the sensation of hundreds of wild fish creates a deeply relaxing experience that commercial urban fish spas cannot replicate. A visit to Ranau Rabbit Farm precedes the scenic drive back to Kota Kinabalu via the PPNS building and the photogenic Floating Mosque before arriving at Tanjung Aru for a South China Sea sunset. Check in to Howard Johnson by Wyndham Kota Kinabalu.
suggestedActivities: Sosodikon Hill pre-dawn sunrise hike (400m, ~10 min walk)
Mount Kinabalu and Crocker Range panoramic views
Desa Cow Farm (bottle-feed calves, fresh dairy tasting)
Natural fish therapy at Bombon Marakau Village river
Return to KK via PPNS building
Howard Johnson by Wyndham KK check-in

days.title: Jesselton Jetty — Sapi Island Snorkelling — Coral Flyer — Manukan Island — Return KK
days.description: After breakfast at the hotel, transfer to Jesselton Jetty for a 09:00 speedboat departure to Sapi Island, one of the finest reef sites in Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park. At Sapi Island, your guide points out the best snorkelling sections of the fringing coral reef — typically the northern point of the island, where visibility is highest and the diversity of reef fish is greatest. Parrotfish, triggerfish, clownfish in their anemone homes, and occasional sea turtles are among the residents. For the adrenaline option, the Coral Flyer zipline harnesses you for a 250-metre flight over the open ocean between Sapi and Gaya Islands at 60 km/h — 90 seconds with the turquoise channel below and the Kota Kinabalu skyline on the horizon. At Gaya Island's sandbar, walk the shoreline at low tide while watching for proboscis monkeys — Borneo's distinctive large-nosed endemic primates — descending from the mangrove forest to the water's edge, a wildlife encounter unique to Borneo that needs no artifice. At 12:00, the speedboat transfers to Manukan Island: a 1,500-metre arc of pristine white sand with calm, clear snorkelling water along the western reef and optional water sports including parasailing, banana boat rides, and scuba diving (own cost). An optional island BBQ lunch is available at RM55 per person. At 14:00, return boat to Jesselton Jetty and transfer to your hotel. In the evening, the local seafood restaurants of Kota Kinabalu await — Todak Restaurant near the waterfront is recommended for its fresh catch priced at market rates.
suggestedActivities: Sapi Island snorkelling (coral reef, parrotfish, triggerfish, turtles)
Gaya Island sandbar walk (proboscis monkey spotting)
Optional parasailing and scuba diving (own cost)
Evening seafood dinner at Todak Restaurant (own cost)

days.title: Mari Mari Cultural Village — Sabah's Five Ethnic Tribes
days.description: The cultural centrepiece of this package. After breakfast at the hotel, transfer approximately 25 minutes from Kota Kinabalu city to Mari Mari Cultural Village — the only site in Sabah, and the first of its kind in all of Southeast Asia, where all five major indigenous ethnic groups of Borneo are represented together in authentic longhouse replicas built by descendants of the tribes themselves. The half-day experience is guided and structured around each tribe's traditional home and skills. The Dusun and Kadazan longhouse introduces Sabah's most numerous ethnic group — rice farmers and traders who developed a complex agricultural calendar around the rice planting cycle and whose female spiritual leaders (bobolian) conducted elaborate rice harvest ceremonies. The Rungus longhouse showcases the distinctive copper jewellery, intricate beadwork, and gong music of this Kadazan-Dusun sub-group. The Lundayeh section covers the hunters and fishermen of the deep interior, whose traditional territory bridges the border with Sarawak and Kalimantan. The Bajau — Sabah's 'sea cowboys' and equally famous as 'sea gypsies' — have one of the most distinctive dual identities in Borneo: fearless horsemen on land and extraordinary free-divers at sea. The Murut section is the most dramatic: the Lansaran, a traditional trampoline of springy bamboo slats historically used to celebrate successful headhunting raids, launches participants several metres into the air in group jumping sessions. Live demonstrations throughout include blowpipe dart-shooting with handcrafted sumpitans, bamboo fire-starting using flint in under 60 seconds, traditional tattoo art, and the betel nut preparation ritual. Lunch is included in the village. Return to Kota Kinabalu in the afternoon for hotel rest, followed by a local restaurant dinner with the Dhesu guide.
suggestedActivities: Transfer to Mari Mari Cultural Village (25 min from KK city)
Dusun/Kadazan longhouse and rice farming heritage
Rungus beadwork and gong music demonstration
Lundayeh hunters' traditions and interior lifestyle
Bajau sea gypsy and horseman culture
Included lunch at Mari Mari Cultural Village

days.title: Museum Negeri Sabah — Filipino Market — Sabah Specialities — Airport Departure
days.description: A final morning of Kota Kinabalu cultural sightseeing and shopping before departure. Begin at Museum Negeri Sabah, one of the finest state museums in Malaysia, with permanent galleries covering Sabah's ethnological heritage — the 30-plus indigenous groups of Borneo, their material culture, traditional costumes, and oral histories — alongside natural history galleries on the ecology of the Crocker Range and the marine environment of the surrounding sea. The museum's grounds include reconstructed traditional longhouses and the Sabah Art Gallery. Continue to Jamilah Jewellery for Sabah's distinctive cultured South Sea pearls, produced in the waters off the east coast, and hand-crafted traditional silverwork. The Filipino Market, a sprawling waterfront market operated largely by Filipino vendors, offers handmade shell crafts, cultured pearls, woven rattan goods, and Moro-style brassware at competitive prices — bargaining is expected and part of the experience. Sabah Choco House introduces the world-class local cacao story: Teck Guan cacao from the Tawau area has won international chocolate awards and the handmade chocolates produced from it — available in an array of local flavours — are among the finest edible souvenirs in all of Malaysia. Final stop is Sabah Layer Cake (kek lapis Sabah): the rainbow steamed layer cakes in dozens of colours and flavours that have been Sabah's most popular take-home gift for generations. Transfer to Kota Kinabalu International Airport for your departure flight.
suggestedActivities: Filipino Market (waterfront crafts, pearls, shell goods)
Sabah Choco House (award-winning Teck Guan cacao chocolates)

whyBook.heading: Sabah's Most Complete Package — Arranged by Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging Sabah journeys for over 35 years. The 5-day Best of KK, Kundasang and Mari Mari package is our most comprehensive Sabah offering — built for travellers who want to leave Borneo having genuinely experienced the state, not just skirted its edges. Our guides at Mari Mari Cultural Village are accredited cultural interpreters who explain the context behind each demonstration; our island day operators know the tide windows and the best reef spots; and our highland logistics ensure you are on Sosodikon Hill before the clouds arrive.

sections.heading: 5 Day Best of Sabah Tour from Malaysia: Kundasang, Marine Park & Mari Mari Cultural Village
sections.body:
A 5-day Best of Sabah tour is the most comprehensive way to experience Borneo's most accessible state in a single trip. This package covers the four distinct landscapes and experiences that define Sabah for serious travellers: the cool highland world of Kundasang (1,700m altitude, Mount Kinabalu National Park, Desa Cow Farm), the island and reef life of Tunku Abdul Rahman National Park just offshore from KK city, the natural highland experience of Bombon Marakau fish therapy and the Ranau valley, and the cultural depth of Mari Mari Cultural Village.

Mari Mari Cultural Village is the defining experience that elevates this package beyond any other Sabah tour. Established on the forested hillsides 25 minutes from Kota Kinabalu city, it is the first venue of its kind in Southeast Asia to bring all five major indigenous ethnic groups of Sabah together in a single site, with longhouses built by descendants of the original communities. The Dusun, Rungus, Lundayeh, Bajau, and Murut peoples each maintain a traditional longhouse with live demonstrations of blowpipe skills, fire-starting, tattoo art, and the remarkable Lansaran bamboo trampoline of the Murut warriors. It is a genuinely irreplaceable experience and the reason most travellers cite this as the best day of their Borneo trip.

For families, the package provides excellent variety across five days — the gentle Kinabalu nature trail and Sosodikon sunrise suit all fitness levels, the Desa Cow Farm is a favourite with younger children, the island snorkelling provides appropriate challenge and excitement for older children, and the Mari Mari demonstrations are engaging for all ages. Group discounts are available from 3 adults, making this Sabah's most cost-effective comprehensive family package.

faq.heading: Questions About This 5-Day Best of Sabah Package

items.question: What are the five ethnic tribes represented at Mari Mari Cultural Village?
items.answer: Mari Mari Cultural Village represents the five major indigenous ethnic groups of Sabah: (1) Dusun/Kadazan — the largest ethnic group in Sabah, traditionally rice farmers and traders with a rich ritual culture centred on the harvest cycle; (2) Rungus — a Kadazan-Dusun sub-group known for distinctive copper jewellery, intricate beadwork, and gong music; (3) Lundayeh — hunters and fishermen from the deep interior, whose traditional territory overlaps with Sarawak and Kalimantan; (4) Bajau — known as Sabah's 'sea cowboys' and 'sea gypsies', remarkable for their horsemanship and extraordinary free-diving ability; (5) Murut — the last Sabahan tribe to renounce headhunting, celebrated as fierce warriors and known for the Lansaran group bamboo trampoline tradition.

items.question: Is Mari Mari Cultural Village suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes, Mari Mari Cultural Village is excellent for children of all ages. The blowpipe demonstration is a favourite with older children and teens — they are invited to try the sumpitan themselves under guide supervision. The bamboo fire-starting demonstration is visually compelling for all ages. The Lansaran bamboo trampoline is exciting to watch. The longhouse interiors and tribal costumes are visually rich and engaging. Children under 12 are generally very well-engaged throughout the 2.5 to 3-hour experience. Lunch is included on this day, which also helps families with young children manage the schedule.

items.question: What is the Lansaran bamboo trampoline?
items.answer: The Lansaran is a traditional Murut amusement device and post-harvest celebration activity: a long platform of flexible bamboo slats suspended between two sturdy frames, designed so that participants standing on the platform can bounce each other — and themselves — several metres into the air through coordinated jumping. Historically, the Lansaran was associated with celebrations following successful headhunting raids and rice harvests. At Mari Mari Cultural Village, Murut community members perform Lansaran jumping demonstrations and visitors are invited to attempt the trampoline under guidance. It is the most physically spectacular demonstration in the village.

items.question: What makes this the most complete Sabah package available?
items.answer: Most Sabah tours focus on one or two themes — highland (Kinabalu) or island (Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park) or cultural (Mari Mari). This 5-day package covers all four: highland nature at Kinabalu National Park and Sosodikon Hill sunrise, pastoral highland at Desa Cow Farm and natural river fish therapy, island and marine at Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park (Sapi and Manukan Islands with snorkelling, Coral Flyer zipline, Gaya Island sandbar), and cultural at Mari Mari Cultural Village. No other standard Sabah package combines all four. The package also includes more meals (breakfast daily, plus lunch and dinner on Day 4) and entrance fees to all sites.

items.question: What are the group discount rates for this 5-day package?
items.answer: The standard price is RM1,678 per person (twin sharing) with an original price of RM2,098. Group discounts apply as follows: 3 or more adults pay RM1,288 per person; 5 or more adults pay RM1,088 per person; 7 or more adults pay RM988 per person. All bookings are 100% private — no joining with other groups. Compulsory tips are RM49 per person. Contact us to confirm availability and secure your group rate.

items.question: What should I pack for a 5-day trip combining highlands, islands, and cultural visits?
items.answer: For the highlands (Days 1–2): a light jacket or windbreaker and long trousers for the Sosodikon Hill sunrise hike (temperatures around 12°C–18°C at dawn), comfortable walking shoes, and a small daypack. For the island day (Day 3): swimwear, rash guard or sun shirt for snorkelling, reef-safe sunscreen, a waterproof dry bag for your phone and valuables, and sandals. For Mari Mari Cultural Village (Day 4): comfortable clothing — light long sleeves are appropriate given that you are a guest in a cultural space; closed-toe shoes are helpful on the village paths. For the city days: light casual clothing for the KK heat (typically 30°C–33°C at sea level). A change bag in the car makes the highland-to-coast transitions easy.
